dictionary.definition: General Financial Rules. A compilation of rules and orders of Government of India to be followed by all offices while dealing with matters involving public finances.
Geological Survey of India. The premier geological organization in India, attached to the Ministry of Mines.
Notice Inviting Tender. A formal notification issued by a department to invite bids for goods or services.
Earnest Money Deposit. A security deposit submitted by bidders along with their bid to ensure they don't withdraw it before the evaluation.
Tender Evaluation Committee. A committee formed to technically and financially evaluate the bids received against an NIT.
Additional Terms and Conditions. Specific conditions added to a GeM bid beyond the standard General Terms and Conditions (GTC).
Government e-Marketplace. An online platform for public procurement in India.
Central Public Procurement Portal. An alternative portal to GeM for publishing tenders.
A certificate issued when only one particular manufacturer's product is required for technical reasons, allowing for single-source procurement under GFR Rule 166.
The maximum monetary value up to which an official or authority can sanction expenditure as per the Delegation of Financial Powers Rules (DFPR).
A mandatory report generated on GeM to check if a product is available on the portal before initiating procurement outside GeM.
